credentials","pros":["Minimal disruption to existing architecture","Reuses tested trait contract and integration test harness","Incremental, reviewable in separate PRs per forge"],"cons":["Some optional integrations"],"cons":["Complicates CI matrix (must test each feature combination)","Runtime forge switching via config becomes harder if forges are conditionally compiled out","Less convenient for users wanting multi-forge support in one binary, which seems to be the issue's intent"],"complexity":"medium","files_touched":10}],"recommendation":"Approach 1 (extend existing Forge trait with per-forge modules) is the pragmatic first step: it follows the precedent set by #735/PR #736, keeps the diff reviewable per forge, and reuses existing test scaffolding. Approach 2 (registry with shared middleware) is the more elegant long-term solution and should be proposed as a fast-follow refactor once GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, and Azure DevOps implementations reveal common duplication worth extracting; recommend landing approach 1 now with a tracked follow-up issue for the registry refactor."}
